Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Continued To Sep-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Administer Decedents Estate Under The Independent Administration Of Estates Act
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Parsons in Propria Persona via Court Call

The Court explained the complexity of Probate without an attorney, stated that Probate is governed by statutes and rules that need to be followed, that handling of estates without the assistance of an attorney may cause delays and strongly encouraged Mr. Parsons to get Counsel. Mr. Parsons stated he is using Nolo Press textbook, and he is willing to seek attorney if needed. The Court informed Mr. Parsons that books do not address complications. Discussion was held with respect to the ages of the grandchildren, their education paths, the issue of the youngest grandchild attending a 2 year college rather than a 4 year college, the possibility of selling the house, and how to complete the inventory and appraisal. The Court advised Mr. Parsons that even if the house is sold, he does not have authority to distribute the funds. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required in light of the waivers. The Court will determine whether it will grant the petition with full or limited authority. Status on filing of Inventory and Appraisal is set for July 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of close of estate is set for March 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court once again strongly encouraged Mr. Parsons to bring an attorney on board.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
